auth.usage_instructions: Authentication management -- login with API key, check status, or logout. Use `auth status` to verify credentials before other operations.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.
login.usage_instructions: Set up API key for Gemini. Use --api-key/-k to pass key directly, or --no-browser to skip opening the browser. Without flags, opens AI Studio in browser and prompts for the key.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

logout.usage_instructions: Remove stored API credentials. Use when rotating keys or switching accounts.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

profiles.usage_instructions: Run `gemini auth profiles --help` for exact arguments and options.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Run `gemini auth profiles create --help` for exact arguments and options.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Run `gemini auth profiles delete --help` for exact arguments and options.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.
get.usage_instructions: Run `gemini auth profiles get --help` for exact arguments and options.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.
list.usage_instructions: Run `gemini auth profiles list --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

remove.help: Delete a profile and its data.

select.help: Activate a profile within its auth type.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to activate within its auth type

status.help: Check authentication status across profiles. Performs a live round-trip for every configured credential type so the report reflects ground truth — not on-disk belief. Saved credentials whose live verification fails are reported as ``authenticated: false`` with the failure reason in ``api_test``.
status.usage_instructions: Verify API key is configured and valid. Use --table/-t for formatted output. Run this first if other commands fail with auth errors.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Run `gemini auth test --help` for exact arguments and options.

cache.usage_instructions: Run `gemini cache --help` for exact arguments and options.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Run `gemini cache clear --help` for exact arguments and options.

chat.usage_instructions: Send prompts to Gemini for text generation and chat. Supports file attachments for multimodal input (images, PDFs).

new.help: Start a new chat conversation with a prompt. Example: gemini chat new "Explain quantum computing in simple terms" gemini chat new "What is in this image?" --file photo.jpg gemini chat new "Summarize this document" -f report.pdf --model gemini-3.1-pro-preview
new.usage_instructions: Send a prompt to Gemini and get a response. PROMPT is required (positional). Attach files with --file/-f (images, PDFs, etc.) for multimodal queries. Use --model/-m to select model (default: gemini-3-pro-preview).

options.name: --file
options.help: File to attach to the conversation

files.help: File operations with Gemini Files API
files.usage_instructions: Manage files uploaded to the Gemini Files API. Upload files for use in chat/video analysis, list uploaded files, get metadata, or delete files. File names use the format `files/abc123`.

delete.help: Delete an uploaded file. Example: gemini files delete files/abc123 gemini files delete files/abc123 --yes
delete.usage_instructions: Remove a file from the Gemini Files API. FILE_NAME is required (positional). Use --yes/-y to skip confirmation prompt.

get.help: Get file metadata. Example: gemini files get files/abc123 gemini files get files/abc123 --table
get.usage_instructions: Retrieve metadata for a specific uploaded file. FILE_NAME is required (positional), in the format `files/abc123`.

list.help: List all uploaded files. Example: gemini files list gemini files list --table
list.usage_instructions: Show all files uploaded to the Gemini Files API. Use --table/-t for formatted table output.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of files to return

upload.help: Upload a file to Gemini Files API. Example: gemini files upload document.pdf gemini files upload video.mp4 --wait
upload.usage_instructions: Upload a local file to Gemini's Files API for later use in prompts. FILE_PATH is required (positional). Use --wait (default) to wait for processing; --no-wait to return immediately for large files.

image.help: Image generation with Nano Banana Pro
image.usage_instructions: Generate images from text prompts. Supports multiple models including gemini-3-pro-image-preview and imagen-4.0. Control aspect ratio, resolution, and enable Google Search grounding for real-time data.

generate.help: Generate an image from a text prompt using Nano Banana Pro. Example: gemini image generate "A cyberpunk cityscape at sunset" gemini image generate "Cloud infrastructure diagram" -o cloud.png -ar 16:9 gemini image generate "Current weather in Tokyo visualization" --search gemini image generate "Professional blog header" -m nano-banana-pro -s 4K
generate.usage_instructions: Create an image from a text description, optionally with reference image(s) for image editing / multi-image composition. PROMPT is required (positional). Use --output/-o to set output path (default: generated_image.png). Use --model/-m to select model (default: gemini-3-pro-image-preview). Set --aspect-ratio/-a (default: 16:9, options: 1:1, 16:9, 9:16, 4:3, 3:4, 1.91:1). Set --size/-s for resolution (default: 2K, options: 1K, 2K, 4K). Use --search for real-time data grounding. Pass one or more reference images with --input-image/-i (repeatable); only Nano Banana models support this (gemini-3-pro-image-preview, gemini-2.5-flash-image) -- Imagen models will reject -i.

arguments.name: prompt
arguments.help: Text prompt describing the image to generate

options.name: --aspect-ratio
options.help: r      [1:1|16:9|9:16|4:3|3:4|1.91:1]                                                        Aspect ratio of generated image

options.name: --search
options.help: Enable Google Search grounding for real-time data

options.name: --input-image
options.help: Reference image(s) for image editing / multi-image composition. Repeatable. Only supported by Nano Banana models (gemini-3-pro-image-preview, gemini-2.5-flash-image)

models.help: List available image generation models. Example: gemini image models
models.usage_instructions: Show which image generation models are available. Useful before `image generate` to pick a model.
Discover available Gemini models for use with chat, image, video, and research commands.

list.help: List available Gemini models. Example: gemini models list gemini models list --table
list.usage_instructions: Show all Gemini models. Use --table/-t for formatted output. Useful for finding model IDs to pass to --model flags in other commands.

research.help: Deep Research using Gemini Deep Research Agent
research.usage_instructions: Run autonomous deep research tasks that plan, search, and synthesize reports with citations. For complex research questions requiring multi-step investigation.

resume.help: Resume streaming a research task that was interrupted. Example: gemini research resume abc123xyz gemini research resume abc123xyz --from-event evt_456
resume.usage_instructions: Continue streaming results from a previously started research task. INTERACTION_ID is required (positional). Use --from-event/-e to resume from a specific event ID.

start.help: Start a deep research task using Gemini Deep Research Agent. The agent autonomously plans, executes, and synthesizes multi-step research tasks. It uses web search and can produce detailed, cited reports. Example: gemini research start "Research the history of Google TPUs" gemini research start "Compare React vs Vue in 2025" --no-stream gemini research start "Analyze Cursor AI features" --timeout 1800 gemini research start --prompt-file /path/to/prompt.txt
start.usage_instructions: Launch a deep research task. PROMPT is optional (positional) -- can also use --prompt-file/-f to read from a file. Results stream by default; use --no-stream to wait for completion. Set --timeout/-t for max wait in seconds (default: 3600). Use --agent/-a to select agent (default: deep-research-pro-preview-12-2025).

options.name: --prompt-file
options.help: Read prompt from file instead of argument

options.name: --timeout
options.help: Maximum time to wait in seconds (default: 60 min)

status.help: Check the status of a running or completed research task. Example: gemini research status abc123xyz
status.usage_instructions: Check progress of a deep research task. INTERACTION_ID is required (positional) -- obtained from `research start` output.

arguments.name: interaction_id
arguments.help: Interaction ID from a previous research task

usage.usage_instructions: Track and manage Gemini API usage. View token counts, costs, and breakdowns by model or day. Supports both local CLI tracking and cloud-based monitoring.

clear.help: Clear all local usage data. Example: gemini usage clear gemini usage clear --yes
clear.usage_instructions: Delete all locally tracked usage data. Use --yes/-y to skip confirmation prompt. Does not affect cloud data.

setup.help: Configure BigQuery billing export for cloud cost tracking. This connects your CLI to Google Cloud's billing export so you can see cost data alongside token usage from Cloud Monitoring. Prerequisites: 1. Enable billing export in GCP Console: Billing > Billing export > BigQuery export > Enable 2. Run: gcloud auth application-default login
setup.usage_instructions: Set up cloud cost tracking via BigQuery billing export. Requires GCP billing export to be enabled and `gcloud auth application-default login` to be run first.

options.name: --save-anyway
options.help: Save the table ID even if connection verification fails

show.help: Show API usage statistics. Use --source local for CLI-only tracking (default). Use --source cloud for ALL Gemini usage across all API consumers via Cloud Monitoring. Example: gemini usage show gemini usage show --source cloud --table gemini usage show --source cloud --by-model --table gemini usage show --source cloud --daily --table --days 7 gemini usage show --days 7 --by-model --table
show.usage_instructions: Display usage stats. Use --source/-s to choose data source: 'local' (CLI-only, default) or 'cloud' (all API consumers via Cloud Monitoring). Use --days/-d to set time range (default: 30). Use --by-model/-m for model breakdown, --daily for daily breakdown. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

options.name: --source
options.help: [local|cloud]  Data source: local (CLI tracking) or cloud (Cloud Monitoring)

video.usage_instructions: Analyze video files using Gemini's multimodal capabilities. Upload and prompt Gemini about video content.

analyze.help: Upload and analyze a video file with Gemini. For videos < 20MB, the video is analyzed immediately using inline upload. For videos >= 20MB, the video is uploaded to Files API first. Provide a prompt either inline with --prompt or from a file with --prompt-file. Additional context files (PDFs, docs, etc.) can be included with --files. Use --json-schema for structured JSON output that guarantees valid JSON. Example: gemini video analyze video.mp4 --prompt "Summarize this video" gemini video analyze video.mp4 --prompt-file prompts/review.txt gemini video analyze lecture.mp4 -p "What are the key topics discussed?" gemini video analyze demo.mp4 -f analysis_prompt.txt --model gemini-3.1-pro-preview gemini video analyze video.mp4 -p "Review this" --files outline.pdf --files notes.txt gemini video analyze video.mp4 -p "Review this" --json-schema schema.json
analyze.usage_instructions: Analyze a video with Gemini. FILE_PATH is required (positional). Provide a prompt with --prompt/-p (inline) or --prompt-file/-f (from file). Videos < 20MB are sent inline; larger files use Files API. Attach extra context files with --files (repeatable). Use --model/-m to select model (default: gemini-3-pro-preview). Use --json-schema/-s for structured JSON output. Set --fps for video sampling rate (default: 1).

options.name: --files
options.help: Additional files to include (can specify multiple times)

options.name: --no-wait
options.help: Don't wait for processing (large files only)

options.name: --json-schema
options.help: Path to JSON schema file for structured output

options.name: --fps
options.help: Frames per second for video sampling (default: 1). Use higher values (2-5) for fast-action or sync detection

usage_instructions: Use for interacting with the Google Gemini API: chat, image generation, video analysis, deep research, file management, and usage tracking. Commands follow `gemini <group> <action>` pattern. Most list commands support --table/-t for formatted output.
